title: Growing Resilient Youth in Transition (GRYT 2.0)

Growing Resilient Youth in Transition (GRYT 2.0)
Share link
Collaboration between Trybe Limited and VCHs to provide aftercare support for youths discharging from VCHs.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based
Free
Referral required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

Growing Resilient Youth in Transition (GRYT 2.0) aims to provide reintegration support for youths who are discharging from Voluntary Children’s Home (VCH). GRYT 2.0 takes a case management, counselling and community-building approach in supporting these youths towards leading purposeful lives and making a positive impact in their community

Who is this for?

Youths, between 13 and 21 years old, who are discharging from a Voluntary Children’s Home (VCH).

What to expect?

Service Users would need to be referred to the programme. Trybe Limited project staff would contact youth for assessment. Duration of sessions would depend on service user's progress.

How to apply?

Service Users need to be referred to the programme by VCHs.

Information last updated on 22 Sep 2025
